task_completion.description: Post 1 is created with the correct title and substantive content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a WordPress post editor or preview showing the title 'The Future of Renewable Energy: A 2026 Outlook' and a body text of at least a few sentences (>200 characters) discussing global renewable energy trends.

task_completion.description: Post 1 has the correct schedule date, category, and tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress editor sidebar or post list showing Post 1 scheduled for July 14, 2026 at 8:00 AM, with the 'News' category selected, and 'featured' and 'guide' in the tags section.

task_completion.description: Post 2 is created with the correct title and substantive content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a WordPress post editor or preview showing the title 'Solar Power Breakthroughs Driving Global Adoption' and a body text of at least a few sentences (>200 characters) discussing solar panel efficiency and adoption.

task_completion.description: Post 2 has the correct schedule date, category, and tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress editor sidebar or post list showing Post 2 scheduled for July 15, 2026 at 8:00 AM, with the 'Technology' category selected, and 'featured' in the tags section.

task_completion.description: Post 3 is created with the correct title and substantive content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a WordPress post editor or preview showing the title 'The Economics of Wind Energy in Coastal Communities' and a body text of at least a few sentences (>200 characters) discussing wind farms and coastal economies.

task_completion.description: Post 3 has the correct schedule date, category, and tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress editor sidebar or post list showing Post 3 scheduled for July 16, 2026 at 8:00 AM, with the 'Technology' category selected, and 'guide' in the tags section.

task_completion.description: All three posts are successfully set to 'Scheduled' status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress 'All Posts' dashboard showing the three specific posts with a 'Scheduled' badge or status next to their titles, or confirmation messages indicating they have been scheduled.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress web GUI to create and schedule the posts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of a web browser navigating the WordPress admin dashboard (wp-admin), rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: The agent generated actual article content rather than copying the prompt instructions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Read the body of the posts in the screenshots. The text should be an actual article (e.g., 'In recent years, renewable energy has seen massive investment...'), NOT the literal prompt text (e.g., 'Write an introductory article about...').

integrity.description: The dates were set using the WordPress scheduling UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Publish' box in the WordPress editor. The date and time should be set in the actual scheduling fields, not just typed into the body of the post.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Post 1 must be titled 'The Future of Renewable Energy: A 2026 Outlook', scheduled for 2026-07-14 08:00:00, category 'news', tags 'featured' and 'guide'. Post 2 must be titled 'Solar Power Breakthroughs Driving Global Adoption', scheduled for 2026-07-15 08:00:00, category 'technology', tag 'featured'. Post 3 must be titled 'The Economics of Wind Energy in Coastal Communities', scheduled for 2026-07-16 08:00:00, category 'technology', tag 'guide'. All posts must have substantive content (>200 characters) and be in 'Scheduled' status.
